steps.title: Find the comparison in the passage
steps.content: The phrase “not as widely spoken as Tzeltal… also has a robust number of speakers” tells us Otomi should be less widely spoken than Tzeltal but still substantial.

steps.title: Match Otomi's speaker count from the table
steps.content: Otomi's entry shows 307,900 speakers in central Mexico, which satisfies this comparison. The other choices are speaker counts for different languages (Me'phaa: 134,100; Rarámuri: 73,856) or use Tzeltal's own count (556,700), which would contradict the comparison. That matches choice C — C is correct.
